title: VM Crash / Hypervisor Fault
symptoms: One or more VMs become unresponsive or crash
Hypervisor host shows red/disconnected state in vCenter/Proxmox
Applications hosted on the VM are unreachable
HA failover triggered (or failed to trigger)
steps: Check VM status in hypervisor management console (vCenter/Proxmox/OpenStack)
Attempt a VM console connection — check for kernel panic, BSOD, or OS-level crash
Check hypervisor host health: CPU, memory, storage I/O on the host running the VM
Review VM event log for the last 30 minutes for hardware errors or storage timeouts
Check datastore health: 'esxcli storage filesystem list' — if storage is degraded, all VMs on it are at risk
Check network connectivity to the VM's virtual switch and portgroup
If VM is running but OS is hung: take a snapshot before rebooting for forensic purposes
If host is physically failed: initiate manual vMotion or HA trigger to move VMs to healthy hosts
resolution: Restart the VM if OS-level crash. If host is failed: trigger HA failover in vCenter or manually migrate VMs. Investigate root cause (storage I/O, memory error, kernel bug). If hardware fault on host: place host in maintenance mode, evacuate VMs, schedule hardware replacement. Document downtime for SLA calculation.
escalation: If a production database or billing system VM is affected, escalate to Cloud/Virtualisation team immediately. If entire host cluster is degraded, page Cloud Architect on-call.
estimated_resolution_time: VM restart: 5-15 minutes. Host failure + migration: 30-90 minutes.
